title: Snakes and Sends at Smith
summary: On our way out of Bend, we stopped at their local used gear store in an unsuccessful search for climbing shoes and warm mid layers. Before we could climb at Smith Rock, we found a camp site at Skull Hollow Campground and on the way listened to an interesting podcast about the history of food regulations (would recommend listening! link). That afternoon, Helen, Tiffany and I did some climbing in Smith Rock and had a great time. The evenings at Skull Hollow Campground were windy and cold, so we spent the nights watching The Dark Wizard documentary on the projector in our van. Our second day at Smith was very hot. Despite the heat, we climbed in direct sunlight on a route called 5 Gallon Buckets. It is one of the most popular routes at Smith and any other day there would be a line to climb it. Because we were braving the sun, we had it all to ourselves and had a great time climbing it! The afternoon was spent recovering at a more shady wall, where we climbed a few pitches and had a gopher snake slither within a few feet of us. Our final day, I went for a run while Helen did some computer work. I ran from our camp ground to Smith and had some great views, but was almost blown off a few summits by the massive wind gusts. We spent the afternoon getting in the remainder of our fill of climbing. We climbed well into the evening, and Helen made us a late dinner of ravioli before bed.
